description: Fires when a visitor is on your site for the selected amount of time (e.g. after 30 seconds or 2 minutes). By default the tag fires on all pages; add conditions to fire only on a specific page or under specific criteria. You must specify the duration using the built-in Time on Website variable (value = number of seconds) and set the condition_type to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session. See Piwik PRO help: Timer trigger (time on website).

time_condition.type: Fire a tag after X seconds/minutes
time_condition.description: Fires the tag when a visitor stays on your site for the selected time.

builtin_variable.name: Time on Website
builtin_variable.description: A variable used in the timer (time on website) trigger. It defines the duration in seconds after which the tag fires.
builtin_variable.usage: Use this variable_id in the condition that defines when the trigger fires. The condition_type for this condition must be set to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session. Set the condition value to the number of seconds (e.g. 30 for 30 seconds, 120 for 2 minutes).
builtin_variable.condition_type_description: For the Time on Website condition, condition_type must be one of these (not equals or other comparison).
builtin_variable.value_description: Number of seconds. E.g. 30 (30 seconds), 60 (1 minute), 120 (2 minutes).

multiplicity: Many times during a session (each time)
time_on_website_conditions_reference.note: The time condition must use the built-in variable 'Time on Website' (id 4a3cbab8-e239-44ca-b1c9-6c1bc5cc3fbd). Set condition_type to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session. Set value to the number of seconds.

condition_types.description: Fires when a visitor stays on a page with the selected page URL for the selected time
Fires when a visitor stays on a page with the selected page path for the selected time
Fires when a visitor stays on a page hosted on the selected domain for the selected time
Fires when a visitor stays on a page to which they were directed by the selected internal referrer for the selected time
Fires when a visitor stays on a page to which they were directed by the selected external referrer for the selected time
Fires when a visitor stays on a page to which they were directed by the selected campaign (utm_campaign) for the selected time
Fires when a visitor stays on a page to which they were directed by the selected traffic source for the selected time. Values: campaign, organic search, social, referral, direct
Fires when a visitor is a returning visitor and stays on a page for the selected time
Any variable can be used to define trigger conditions

when_to_use: Fire a tag after a visitor has been on the page or site for a set duration
Show messages or surveys after X seconds of engagement
Track time-on-page or delayed conversion signals
common_use_cases: Fire tag after 30 seconds on page
Show exit-intent or offer after 2 minutes
Engagement or survey after 60 seconds on specific pages
best_practices: Set value to the number of seconds (e.g. 30, 60, 120)
Use once_per_page_view or once_per_session to avoid repeated firing; use each_time if you need multiple firings per page/session
Add Page path or Page URL conditions to limit where the timer runs

create_trigger.description: Create a timer (time on website) trigger. Requires at least one condition using the built-in 'Time on Website' variable (variable_id 4a3cbab8-e239-44ca-b1c9-6c1bc5cc3fbd) with condition_type set to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session and value set to the number of seconds; optionally add event conditions.

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Include at least one condition with variable_id 4a3cbab8-e239-44ca-b1c9-6c1bc5cc3fbd (Time on Website), condition_type = each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session, value = number of seconds. Optional event conditions.

update_trigger.description: Update an existing timer trigger – only editable fields will be processed

conditions.description: Array of condition objects. Must include at least one condition using the built-in variable 'Time on Website' (variable_id 4a3cbab8-e239-44ca-b1c9-6c1bc5cc3fbd) with condition_type = each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session and value = number of seconds. May include event conditions.

structure.variable_id: For timer duration use 4a3cbab8-e239-44ca-b1c9-6c1bc5cc3fbd (Time on Website); for event conditions use the appropriate variable UUID
structure.condition_type: For the Time on Website condition must be one of: each_time, once_per_page_view, once_per_session. For event conditions use the appropriate type (e.g. equals, contains).
structure.value: Number of seconds when using Time on Website variable (e.g. 30, 60, 120)

conditions.recommendation: Include one condition with variable_id 4a3cbab8-e239-44ca-b1c9-6c1bc5cc3fbd (Time on Website), condition_type each_time | once_per_page_view | once_per_session, and value = seconds; add Page path or Page URL conditions to limit where the trigger fires.

time_on_website_30_seconds.description: Fire after 30 seconds on page (each time)

time_on_website_60_seconds_once_per_page.description: Fire once per page view after 60 seconds

time_on_website_120_seconds_once_per_ses.description: Fire once per session after 2 minutes (120 seconds)

time_on_website_with_page_path.description: Fire after 45 seconds only on blog pages

common_mistakes: Using condition_type other than each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session for the Time on Website condition
Setting value in minutes instead of seconds (use seconds, e.g. 120 for 2 minutes)
Omitting the Time on Website condition so the trigger has no duration

possible_causes: condition_type not set to each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session for the Time on Website condition
Value not a valid number of seconds
debugging_steps: Ensure one condition uses variable_id 4a3cbab8-e239-44ca-b1c9-6c1bc5cc3fbd with condition_type each_time, once_per_page_view, or once_per_session and value = seconds
Use a small value (e.g. 5) to test; ensure the user stays on the page long enough
Test with only the Time on Website condition
